context: Initial tool-use policy for the agent platform. Defines which external services the agent is authorized to call and the default behavior when no policy applies.
decision: Adopt tool-use policy v1: the agent may call the approved service list; any unlisted service requires an explicit per-request authorization record.
options: Allow-list of approved services plus per-request authorization for others
Deny-list of forbidden services with open default
rationale: An allow-list gives a clear, auditable default and avoids the need to enumerate all possible misuse categories up front.

context: Review following an external audit finding that policy-tooluse-v1 did not explicitly address outbound network access from shell-like tools. The audit recommended an explicit statement and a review cadence.
decision: Adopt tool-use policy v2: retain the allow-list from v1 and add an explicit clause requiring outbound network access from shell-like tools to be logged and reviewed on a quarterly cadence.
options: Adopt v2 with the explicit clause and quarterly review
Adopt v2 with the explicit clause but no review cadence
rationale: The explicit clause closes the audit finding. The quarterly review cadence is lightweight and creates a durable checkpoint.

context: Targeted revision following the quarterly review introduced in policy-tooluse-v2. Review identified that two services on the allow-list had been deprecated upstream.
decision: Adopt tool-use policy v3: remove the two deprecated services from the allow-list; retain all other clauses from v2 unchanged.
options: Remove deprecated services and tighten the allow-list further
Defer until the next full review
rationale: Deprecated services cannot be reached and leaving them on the allow-list creates stale references. A broader tightening was considered but deferred to the next scheduled review.
